sections.title: Del 1 - Lytting og lesing
sections.description: Du har 1 time på denne delen. Her skal du vise at du kan forstå muntlig og skriftlig informasjon.

tasks.title: What is the purpose of this announcement?
tasks.context: Recording: Plastic Reduction - Single-use plastics are destroying our oceans. The Ocean Clean Initiative works to reduce plastic waste through innovative recycling programs and community education. If you want to learn more about how to reduce your plastic consumption, visit our website or call 855 300 400. Our volunteers are ready to help you make a difference.

options.label: To warn and discourage
To educate and encourage action
To sell recycling equipment
To discourage ocean activities

tasks.explanation: Kunngjøringen formidlar informasjon og oppfordrar folk til å ta handling gjennom å kontakte organisasjonen eller besøkje nettsida deira.

tasks.title: What is the primary benefit mentioned?
tasks.context: Recording: Solar Energy - Today we are launching a new initiative to provide solar panels to low-income families. Our studies show that solar energy can reduce electricity costs by 50 percent. Installation will begin next month in the northern districts. All interested residents should register online at greenenergystart.org. Limited slots are available.

options.label: Solar panels are free for everyone
Solar energy reduces electricity costs
All families must install solar panels
Installation starts immediately

tasks.explanation: Opptaket eksplisitt seier at solenergi kan redusera elektrisitetskostnadene med 50 prosent.

tasks.title: The text is mainly about...
tasks.context:
Scientists discovering a new island full of plastic.
A researcher's breakthrough in ocean cleanup technology.
Why ocean plastic is impossible to remove.
Young people's responsibility for environmental problems.

Dr. Harrison had just received news of a breakthrough in reducing ocean plastic. She sat down at her desk with a mixture of emotions. After twenty years of research, her team had finally developed a method to clean the Great Pacific Garbage Patch more efficiently. This massive accumulation of plastic waste, often called the "eighth continent," had haunted her for decades.

options.label: Scientists discovering a new island full of plastic
A researcher's breakthrough in ocean cleanup technology
Why ocean plastic is impossible to remove
Young people's responsibility for environmental problems

tasks.explanation: Teksten fokuserer på Dr. Harrison sitt gjennombrudd innanfor rensing av hav-plast etter 20 år med forsking.

tasks.title: What does Dr. Harrison believe is the bigger challenge?

options.label: Cleaning the existing plastic in the ocean
Preventing new plastic from entering the ocean
Testing the new technology
Convincing other scientists

tasks.explanation: I teksten står det at "the real victory would come only when society fundamentally changed its relationship with plastic."

tasks.title: Click on the phrase which means the same as "help"
tasks.context: Peter is a popular guy and he always shows up when friends ask him to. Even though he isn't loaded he never shows up empty-handed when he is visiting his friends. He brings gifts and food. So on his birthday his friends decided to pitch in and have a surprise party for him.
tasks.explanation: "Pitch in" tyder å bidra eller hjelpe til. Det passar perfekt i denne konteksten der vennene hans bidreg til ein bursdagsfest for honom.

tasks.title: According to the text, which statement is true?
tasks.context: According to a recent study by the International Energy Agency, countries with strong government support for renewable energy have seen a faster transition away from fossil fuels. Investment in wind and solar infrastructure has grown exponentially over the past decade. These investments have created millions of jobs globally while simultaneously reducing carbon emissions. The study concludes that policy-driven incentives are crucial for widespread renewable energy adoption.

options.label: Government support slows down renewable energy adoption
Government support speeds up the transition to renewable energy
Renewable energy develops at the same rate regardless of government policy
Government support is less important than consumer demand

tasks.explanation: Teksten seier eksplisitt at "countries with strong government support... have seen a faster transition away from fossil fuels."

tasks.title: What is the primary advantage of smart irrigation systems?
tasks.context: Smart irrigation systems represent a significant breakthrough in agriculture. These systems use sensors to detect soil moisture levels and automatically adjust water flow accordingly. Farmers using this technology have reported water savings of up to 40 percent while maintaining the same crop yields. In drought-prone regions, smart irrigation has become essential for sustainable agriculture.

options.label: They increase crop production significantly
They reduce water consumption while maintaining productivity
They are suitable only for large farms
They eliminate the need for human labor

tasks.explanation: Systemet sparer opp til 40 prosent vatn medan det opprettheld same produksjonen av grodsaker.

sections.title: Del 2 - Videreformidling av innhold
sections.description: Du skal kort videreformidle informasjon og innhold i noe du leser eller hører. Denne oppgaven blir vurdert av sensor.

tasks.title: The Green City Initiative
tasks.instruction: You are going to listen to a story about how two environmental activists transformed their neighborhood into a sustainable community. Read the task, then listen to the recording, making notes for your text.
tasks.context: This week's topic on your school's webpage is 'Sustainability in Action'. You have been asked to retell the story about how a community reduced its carbon footprint.
requirements: The results and changes they achieved
tasks.placeholder: Skriv ein kort oppsummering av historien. Dekk hovudpoengane...

rubric.clarity: Teksten er tydelig og lett å forstå

sections.title: Del 3 - Samhandling
sections.description: Du skal respondere kort på noe du leser eller hører. Her uttrykker du både egne synspunkter og viser til det andre sier. Denne oppgaven blir vurdert av sensor.

tasks.title: Individual vs Collective Action
tasks.instruction: The people below have written their opinions on environmental responsibility. Read the text and answer the task below.

posts.author: EcoWarrior_22
posts.content: I am so tired of hearing people say they cannot make a difference individually. Every action counts! I have changed my lifestyle completely - I use public transport, buy only secondhand clothes, and I'm vegetarian. My friends think I'm extreme, but I don't care. If everyone did what I do, we wouldn't have a climate crisis. Individual choices matter more than waiting for governments to act.

posts.author: SustainableSkeptic_19
posts.content: EcoWarrior, I appreciate your passion, but I think you're being unrealistic. Individual actions feel good, but they're just drops in the ocean. The real problem is that 70 percent of global emissions come from just 100 companies. No matter how much I recycle or cycle to school, it won't matter if corporations don't change. We need systemic change, not personal guilt trips.

posts.author: GreenOptimist_20
posts.content: I think you both have valid points. Yes, corporations are responsible for most emissions, but individual choices still matter. When millions of people make sustainable choices, it creates demand for green products and policies. I started small by reducing my plastic use, and it's motivated others around me to do the same. Change happens at both personal and systemic levels.

tasks.task: Write the next post in the forum above, and express your own opinion on whether individual environmental actions or systemic change is more important. Make sure to refer to some of the other posts. Comment on, criticise, compliment and/or question what has already been written.
tasks.placeholder: Skriv ditt eige foruminnlegg her. Vær sikker på å referere til dei andre innlegga...

rubric.relevance: Svarar på spørsmålet og rifererer til andre innlegg
rubric.engagement: Kommenterer, kritiserer, eller komplimenterer dei andre
rubric.argumentation: Presenterer ein klar og grunngjeven mening

sections.title: Del 4 - Skriftlig produksjon
sections.description: Velg berre ein av oppgavene: enten 26A, 26B, 26C eller 26D. Du skal skrive ein tekst der du viser evne til kritisk tenking og refleksjon. Denne oppgaven blir vurdert av sensor.

tasks.title: The Cost of Sustainability
tasks.instruction: In the preparation material you have read about the economic and social costs of transitioning to sustainable practices. Write a text in which you explore whether the benefits of sustainability outweigh the costs to society.
tasks.requirements: Refer to examples from the preparation material and/or other sources where relevant.
tasks.placeholder: Skriv din essay her. Vær sikker på å presentere både argument og eksempel...

rubric.content: Utforskar spørsmålet grundig med konkrete eksempel
rubric.argumentation: Presenterer balanserte argument for og imot
rubric.clarity: Teksten er organisert og lett å forstå

tasks.title: Technology and the Environment
tasks.instruction: Write a text in which you argue for and/or against the following statement: "Technology alone cannot solve environmental problems. We need fundamental changes in human behavior and values."
tasks.requirements: Refer to examples from the preparation material and/or other sources where relevant.
tasks.placeholder: Skriv your argument her. Vær sikker på å bruke konkrete eksempel...

rubric.position: Tek ei klar stilling til påstanden

tasks.title: Youth and Climate Action
tasks.instruction: Climate change disproportionately affects younger generations. Write a text in which you discuss how young people can create meaningful environmental change and what support they need from society.
tasks.requirements: Refer to examples from the preparation material and/or other sources where relevant.
tasks.placeholder: Diskuter ungdomars rolle i miljøendring og korleis dei kan få støtte...

tasks.title: The Responsibility of Companies
tasks.instruction: Many argue that large corporations have a greater responsibility for environmental change than individuals. Write a text in which you reflect on the balance of responsibility between companies, governments, and individuals in creating a sustainable future.
tasks.requirements: Refer to examples from the preparation material and/or other sources where relevant.
tasks.placeholder: Reflekter over ansvarsfåordeling mellom bedrifter, statar og individ...

rubric.synthesis: Kombinerar ide til eit heilskapseild argument
